title: Urogenital embryology
body_html:
Kidneys

Pronephros
(rudimentary and non-functional)
↓
Mesonephros
(appears in 4th week, function as interim kidneys)
↓
Metanephros
(starts to function around 9-10th week)

The metanephros develops into two main sources, the ureteric bud and the metanephrogenic blastema:

Ureteric bud
  • gives rise to the: ureter, renal pelvis, collecting ducts and calyces

Metanephrogenic blastema
  • gives rise to the glomerulus and renal tubules up to and including the distal convoluted tubule

Genitals

Mesonephric duct (Wolffian duct) - in men only - Sertoli cells of testes secrete Mullerian-inhibiting factor (see below)
  • Seminal vesicles
  • Epididymis
  • Ejaculatory duct
  • Ductus deferens
  • SEED

Paramesonephric duct (Mullerian duct) - in women only - by default mesonephric duct degenerates (see above)
  • fallopian tube
  • uterus
  • upper third of the vagina

MalesFemales
Genital tubercleGlans penis

Corpus cavernosum and spongiosum		Glans clitoris

Vestibular bulbs
Urogenital sinusBulbourethral glands

Prostate gland		Bartholin glands

Skene glands
Urogenital foldsVentral shaft of penisLabia minora
Labioscrotal swellingScrotumLabia majora
